topic: Immunophenotyping of Hematopoietic Cells by Flow Cytometry
scope: Expected surface and cytoplasmic expression of clinically utilized flow-cytometry markers across all normal human hematopoietic maturation stages (myeloid, B, T, NK, plasma cell) and relevant non-hematolymphoid populations in blood, marrow, and lymphoid tissue -- the normal immunophenotypic template used in diagnostic hematopathology.

papers.title: Immunophenotypic analysis of hematogones (B-lymphocyte precursors) in 662 consecutive bone marrow specimens by 4-color flow cytometry
papers.authors: R W McKenna, L T Washington, D B Aquino, L J Picker, S H Kroft
papers.inclusion_reason: The foundational large-series study defining hematogone immunophenotype and its normal antigenic evolution across age groups by 4-color flow cytometry; the reference point for every later hematogone-vs-lymphoblast discriminator paper. No PMC open-access version exists; included as the seminal primary source.

papers.title: Immunophenotypic analysis of hematogones (B-lymphocyte precursors) and neoplastic lymphoblasts by 4-color flow cytometry
papers.authors: Robert W McKenna, Sheryl L Asplund, Steven H Kroft
papers.inclusion_reason: Direct head-to-head comparison of hematogone antigen-expression patterns against neoplastic B-lymphoblasts, formalizing the aberrancy-based discriminator approach that underlies routine MRD/regenerating-marrow workups. No PMC open-access version exists; included as a foundational companion to the 2001 series.

papers.title: Flow cytometric analysis of human bone marrow. II. Normal B lymphocyte development
papers.authors: M R Loken, V O Shah, K L Dattilio, C I Civin
papers.inclusion_reason: The original description of sequential CD10/CD20/CD34 (and downstream CD21/CD22) acquisition/loss defining the B-cell maturation continuum in bone marrow; the antigen-sequence backbone every subsequent hematogone/B-precursor gating scheme builds on. No PMC open-access version exists; included as the field-defining primary source.

papers.title: Differential expression of CD200 in B-cell neoplasms by flow cytometry can assist in diagnosis, subclassification, and bone marrow staging
papers.authors: Pramoda Challagundla, L Jeffrey Medeiros, Rashmi Kanagal-Shamanna, Roberto N Miranda, Jeffrey L Jorgensen
papers.inclusion_reason: Establishes CD200 expression patterns across B-lineage neoplasms and normal B-cell populations (including hematogones), underpinning CD200 as a discriminator marker now standard in B-cell immunophenotyping panels. No PMC open-access version exists; included as the primary source for this discriminator.

papers.title: Lack of surface immunoglobulin light chain expression by flow cytometric immunophenotyping can help diagnose peripheral B-cell lymphoma
papers.authors: Shiyong Li, James R Eshleman, Michael J Borowitz
papers.inclusion_reason: Foundational demonstration that surface kappa/lambda light-chain restriction (and its absence) by flow cytometry is a clonality marker for mature B-cell neoplasms, the basis of routine light-chain-restriction assessment in every B-cell flow panel. No PMC open-access version exists; included as the primary methodological source.

papers.title: The patterns and diagnostic significance of the lack of surface immunoglobulin light chain on mature B cells in clinical samples for lymphoma workup
papers.authors: Sara L Huang, Thomas Fennell, Xia Chen, James Z Huang
papers.inclusion_reason: Recent, authoritative update on light-chain-restriction assessment, categorizing four distinct patterns of abnormal/absent surface immunoglobulin expression and clarifying when cytoplasmic light chain must supplement surface staining -- brings the classic 2002 discriminator paper's approach up to current practice.

papers.title: Human B-cell subset identification and changes in inflammatory diseases
papers.authors: Rebekah L Velounias, Thomas J Tull
papers.inclusion_reason: Recent open-access review synthesizing mature B-cell subset immunophenotyping by flow cytometry (naive, memory, marginal-zone-like, germinal-center-derived) via CD27/IgD/IgM and related markers -- the authoritative modern reference for the mature-B-cell side of this subarea, available in full via PMC.

papers.title: Novel Strategy for Phenotypic Characterization of Human B Lymphocytes from Precursors to Effector Cells by Flow Cytometry
papers.authors: Giovanna Clavarino, Noemie Delouche, Claire Vettier, David Laurin, Martine Pernollet, Tatiana Raskovalova, Jean-Yves Cesbron, Chantal Dumestre-Perard, Marie-Christine Jacob
papers.inclusion_reason: Open-access unified gating strategy spanning the full B-cell continuum from bone marrow precursors through mature/memory/plasmablast effector stages in a single multiparameter panel, bridging the precursor (hematogone) and mature-subset ends of this subarea.

papers.title: Flow cytometric analysis of human bone marrow. III. Neutrophil maturation
papers.authors: Terstappen LW, Safford M, Loken MR
papers.inclusion_reason: Foundational Terstappen/Loken multidimensional flow-cytometry study defining the six-stage myeloblast-to-segmented-neutrophil maturation sequence via CD11b/CD15/CD16 and light scatter; no open-access equivalent found, included paywalled as the field's origin paper.

papers.title: Immunophenotypic differentiation patterns of normal hematopoiesis in human bone marrow: Reference patterns for age-related changes and disease-induced shifts
papers.authors: van Lochem EG, van der Velden VH, Wind HK, te Marvelde JG, Westerdaal NA, van Dongen JJ
papers.inclusion_reason: The canonical multi-lineage reference-pattern paper (Euroflow/van Dongen group) mapping normal granulocytic, monocytic, and other hematopoietic maturation trajectories used as the diagnostic baseline across the field; marked free article on PubMed.

papers.title: Flow cytometric immunophenotypic differentiation patterns of bone marrow eosinophilopoiesis
papers.authors: Trindade CJ, Sun X, Maric D, Sharma S, Komarow HD, Hourigan CS, Klion A, Maric I
papers.inclusion_reason: Recent, open-access (PMC) authoritative study specifically mapping eosinophil maturation immunophenotype (CD9, CD13, CD33, CD66b, HLA-DR loss) by flow cytometry, filling the eosinophilopoiesis gap in the granulocytic maturation sequence.

papers.title: Introduction to the diagnosis and classification of monocytic-lineage leukemias by flow cytometry
papers.authors: Matarraz S, Almeida J, Flores-Montero J, Lecrevisse Q, Guerri V, Lopez A, Barrena S, van der Velden VH, te Marvelde JG, van Dongen JJ, Orfao A
papers.inclusion_reason: Authoritative EuroFlow-group review of normal monocytic maturation immunophenotype (CD14, CD64, CD36, HLA-DR acquisition from promonocyte to mature monocyte) and its relevance to disease classification; marked free article on PubMed.

papers.title: Immunophenotypic study of basophils by multiparameter flow cytometry
papers.authors: Han X, Jorgensen JL, Brahmandam A, Schlette E, Huh YO, Shi Y, Awagu S, Chen W
papers.inclusion_reason: Foundational multiparameter flow-cytometry characterization of the normal basophil immunophenotype (CD9, CD13, CD22, CD25, CD33, CD36, CD38, CD123 bright, HLA-DR negative), the primary reference for basophil identification by flow; no open-access equivalent found.

papers.title: Identification of CD203c as a New Basophil-Specific Flow-Marker in Ph+ Chronic Myeloid Leukemia
papers.authors: Sadovnik I, Ivanov D, Smiljkovic D, Stefanzl G, Degenfeld-Schonburg L, Herndlhofer S, Eisenwort G, Hauswirth AW, Sliwa T, Keil F, Sperr WR, Valent P
papers.inclusion_reason: Open-access (PMC) recent study validating CD203c as a specific basophil-lineage marker by flow cytometry, extending the basophil immunophenotyping panel beyond CD123/HLA-DR-negative gating and directly relevant to disease-associated basophil identification.

papers.title: Human natural killer cells
papers.authors: Michael A. Caligiuri
papers.inclusion_reason: Foundational ASH 50th-anniversary review establishing the CD56bright/CD16dim (regulatory/cytokine-producing) versus CD56dim/CD16bright (cytotoxic) NK-cell subset framework that underlies all flow-cytometric NK immunophenotyping panels.

papers.title: Expression patterns of NKG2A, KIR, and CD57 define a process of CD56dim NK-cell differentiation uncoupled from NK-cell education
papers.authors: Niklas K. Björkström, Peggy Riese, Frank Heuts, Sandra Andersson, Cyril Fauriat, Martin A. Ivarsson, Andreas T. Björklund, Malin Flodström-Tullberg, Jakob Michaëlsson, Martin E. Rottenberg, Carlos A. Guzmán, Hans-Gustaf Ljunggren, Karl-Johan Malmberg
papers.inclusion_reason: Defines the sequential NKG2A-loss / KIR-acquisition / CD57-acquisition maturation trajectory of CD56dim NK cells, establishing CD57 as the canonical terminal-maturation marker used in NK flow panels.

papers.title: Flow Cytometric Immunophenotyping Is Sensitive for the Early Diagnosis of De Novo Aggressive Natural Killer Cell Leukemia (ANKL): A Multicenter Retrospective Analysis
papers.authors: Yi Li, Jia Wei, Xia Mao, Qingping Gao, Longlong Liu, Ping Cheng, Limei Liu, Xinhua Zhang, Ke Zhang, Jin Wang, Li Zhu, Jianfeng Zhou, Yicheng Zhang, Li Meng, Hanying Sun, Dengju Li, Mei Huang, Wei Huang, Jinniu Deng, Donghua Zhang
papers.inclusion_reason: Multicenter study characterizing the distinctive CD56bright/CD16dim, CD57-negative, reduced-KIR immunophenotype of aggressive NK-cell leukemia versus other NK proliferative disorders, directly applying the CD16/CD56/CD57/KIR marker set to malignant diagnosis.

papers.title: Phenotypic profile of expanded NK cells in chronic lymphoproliferative disorders: a surrogate marker for NK-cell clonality
papers.authors: Paloma Bárcena, María Jara-Acevedo, María Dolores Tabernero, Antonio López, María Luz Sánchez, Andrés C. García-Montero, Noemí Muñoz-García, María Belén Vidriales, Artur Paiva, Quentin Lecrevisse, Margarida Lima, Anton W. Langerak, Sebastian Böttcher, Jacques J. M. van Dongen, Alberto Orfao, Julia Almeida
papers.inclusion_reason: Lima/Orfao-group study using KIR (CD158a/b/e) and NKG2A restriction patterns by flow cytometry as a surrogate marker for NK-cell clonality -- the direct methodological basis for using KIR repertoire skewing to assess NK-cell neoplasms.

papers.title: Establishing NK-Cell Receptor Restriction by Flow Cytometry and Detecting Potential NK-Cell Clones of Uncertain Significance
papers.authors: Jansen N. Seheult, Gregory E. Otteson, Dragan Jevremovic, Pedro Horna, Michael M. Timm, Ji Yuan, William G. Morice, Horatiu Olteanu, Min Shi
papers.inclusion_reason: Recent authoritative reference-interval study defining discriminatory cutoffs for CD158a/CD158b/CD158e/KIR-negative and NKG2A restriction by flow cytometry to distinguish NK-cell neoplasms from reactive NK expansions -- the current clinical standard for KIR/NKG2A-based NK clonality assessment.

papers.title: Analysis of the KIR repertoire in human NK cells by flow cytometry
papers.authors: Niklas K. Björkström, Cyril Fauriat, Yenan T. Bryceson, Johan K. Sandberg, Hans-Gustaf Ljunggren, Karl-Johan Malmberg
papers.venue: Methods in Molecular Biology, vol. 612, pp. 353-364 (in: Natural Killer Cell Protocols, ed. K. Campbell, Humana Press)
papers.inclusion_reason: Methodological protocol chapter detailing multicolor flow-cytometric panel design and gating strategy for resolving the KIR repertoire (CD158a/h, CD158b, CD158e/k) on human NK cells -- the technical reference underlying KIR-based clonality/repertoire assessment.

papers.title: High-Sensitivity Flow Cytometric Analysis for the Evaluation of Systemic Mastocytosis Including the Identification of a New Flow Cytometric Criterion for Bone Marrow Involvement
papers.authors: Olga Pozdnyakova, Svetlana Kondtratiev, Betty Li, Karry Charest, David M. Dorfman
papers.inclusion_reason: Foundational paper establishing CD117-bright mast cell gating and aberrant CD2/CD25 co-expression as flow criteria for systemic mastocytosis bone marrow involvement; no open-access equivalent identified, PubMed record used for verified bibliographic data.

papers.title: Multicolor Flow Cytometric Immunophenotyping Is Highly Sensitive and Specific in Identifying Aberrant Mast Cells in the Diagnostic Workup of Systemic Mastocytosis
papers.authors: Okechukwu Valentine Nwogbo, Hong Fang, Wei Wang, Jie Xu, Roberto N. Miranda, Prithviraj Bose, Chi Young Ok, Jeffrey L. Jorgensen, L. Jeffrey Medeiros, Sa A. Wang
papers.inclusion_reason: Large contemporary MD Anderson series validating multicolor flow criteria (CD117, CD25, CD2, CD30) for aberrant mast cells against morphology/molecular gold standards; no PMC open-access version found, included as the current diagnostic-performance benchmark.

papers.title: Immunophenotypic Characterization of Reactive and Neoplastic Plasmacytoid Dendritic Cells Permits Establishment of a 10-Color Flow Cytometric Panel for Initial Workup and Residual Disease Evaluation of Blastic Plasmacytoid Dendritic Cell Neoplasm
papers.authors: Wei Wang, Joseph D. Khoury, Roberto N. Miranda, Jeffrey L. Jorgensen, Jie Xu, Sanam Loghavi, Shaoying Li, Naveen Pemmaraju, Than Nguyen, L. Jeffrey Medeiros, Sa A. Wang
papers.inclusion_reason: Open-access foundational study defining a CD123/CD303/CD304/HLA-DR-based flow panel that distinguishes reactive from neoplastic pDCs and enables MRD detection in BPDCN.

papers.title: Flow Cytometry Identifies a Spectrum of Maturation in Myeloid Neoplasms Having Plasmacytoid Dendritic Cell Differentiation
papers.authors: Fatima Hamadeh, Amad Awadallah, Howard J. Meyerson, Rose C. Beck
papers.inclusion_reason: Characterizes the immunophenotypic spectrum of CD56-negative pDC proliferations within myeloid neoplasms, an important pitfall distinguishing reactive/maturing pDCs from BPDCN; no PMC open-access version found (Wiley paywall), included for its distinct maturation-spectrum data.

papers.title: Delineation of Erythropoietic Intermediates by Flow Cytometry
papers.authors: Kathleen E. McGrath
papers.inclusion_reason: Recent open-access review synthesizing CD71/CD36/CD105/CD235a-based gating schemes that resolve sequential erythroid maturation stages, giving authoritative modern context for the accessory erythroid-precursor panel.

papers.title: Immunophenotypic Analysis of Erythroid Dysplasia in Myelodysplastic Syndromes. A Report from the IMDSFlow Working Group
papers.authors: Theresia M. Westers, Arjan A. van der Loosdrecht, et al. (IMDSFlow Working Group)
papers.inclusion_reason: Open-access multicenter consensus study standardizing CD71/CD105/CD36/CD235a-based flow assessment of erythroid maturation and dysplasia, the field's reference framework for erythroid precursor immunophenotyping in marrow flow panels.

papers.title: Altered Immunophenotypic Features of Peripheral Blood Platelets in Myelodysplastic Syndromes
papers.authors: Alex F. Sandes, Mihoko Yamamoto, Sergio Matarraz, Maria de Lourdes L.F. Chauffaille, Sandra Quijano, Antonio Lopez, Tsutomu Oguro, Eliza Y.S. Kimura, Alberto Orfao
papers.inclusion_reason: Open-access foundational study defining CD41/CD61/CD36/CD31/CD42b-based flow immunophenotypic abnormalities of platelets/megakaryocyte lineage as a diagnostic aid in myeloid neoplasms.

papers.title: Flow Cytometry Immunophenotyping of Healthy Platelets and Hospitalized Patients with Suspected Platelet Dysfunction: Challenges for Establishing a Cutoff Value
papers.authors: Daiane Keller Cecconello, Fabiane Spagnol, Ana Paula Alegretti, Diogo Andre Pilger, Mariela Granero Farias
papers.inclusion_reason: Open-access contemporary study establishing normal-range flow cytometric expression of CD41a/CD61/CD9/CD42b on platelets, providing the healthy-baseline reference against which marrow/blood megakaryocyte-lineage aberrancy is judged.

papers.title: Flow cytometric disease monitoring in multiple myeloma: the relationship between normal and neoplastic plasma cells predicts outcome after transplantation
papers.authors: Andy C. Rawstron, Faith E. Davies, Ranjit DasGupta, A. John Ashcroft, Russell Patmore, Mark T. Drayson, et al.
papers.inclusion_reason: Foundational paper first demonstrating clinical utility of flow-cytometric MRD monitoring in myeloma via normal-vs-neoplastic plasma cell discrimination (CD45/CD38/CD138/CD19/CD56); no open PMC full text found, but no equivalent open substitute covers this founding result.

papers.title: New criteria to identify risk of progression in monoclonal gammopathy of uncertain significance and smoldering multiple myeloma based on multiparameter flow cytometry analysis of bone marrow plasma cells
papers.authors: Ernesto Pérez-Persona, María-Belén Vidriales, Gema Mateo, Ramón García-Sanz, Maria-Victoria Mateos, Alfonso García de Coca, Josefina Galende, Guillermo Martín-Nuñez, José M. Alonso, Natalia de Las Heras, José M. Hernández, Alejandro Martín, Consuelo López-Berges, Alberto Orfao, Jesús F. San Miguel
papers.inclusion_reason: San Miguel/Spanish-group landmark establishing that the ratio of aberrant-to-normal bone marrow plasma cells by flow cytometry risk-stratifies MGUS/SMM progression, a core clinical application of plasma cell immunophenotyping; no open PMC version found.

papers.title: Immunophenotype of normal vs. myeloma plasma cells: Toward antibody panel specifications for MRD detection in multiple myeloma
papers.authors: Juan Flores-Montero, Ruth de Tute, Bruno Paiva, José Juan Perez, Sebastian Böttcher, Henk Wind, Luzalba Sanoja, Noemí Puig, Quentin Lecrevisse, María Belén Vidriales, Jacques J. M. van Dongen, Alberto Orfao
papers.inclusion_reason: Core EuroFlow reference detailing the normal-vs-clonal plasma cell immunophenotype (CD38, CD138, CD45, CD19, CD56, CD27, CD81, CD117, cyKappa/cyLambda) underlying the EuroFlow PCD/NGF antibody panel; no open PMC full text found, included via publisher DOI.

papers.title: Immunophenotyping of plasma cells
papers.authors: Andy C. Rawstron
papers.venue: Current Protocols in Cytometry, Chapter 6, Unit 6.23
papers.inclusion_reason: Rawstron's foundational methods reference standardizing plasma cell gating and marker interpretation (CD38, CD138, CD45, CD19, CD56, cytoplasmic light chains) that underpins subsequent clinical panels; no open PMC version found.

papers.title: Current applications of multiparameter flow cytometry in plasma cell disorders
papers.authors: Tomas Jelinek, Renata Bezdekova, Michal Zatopkova, Lucia Burgos, Michal Simicek, Tereza Sevcikova, Bruno Paiva, Roman Hajek
papers.inclusion_reason: Open-access, authoritative review synthesizing plasma cell marker panels (CD38, CD138, CD19, CD27, CD56, CD81, CD117, BCMA/CD269) and their diagnostic/prognostic use across MGUS, SMM, and MM; open PMC full text.

papers.title: Flow cytometric immunophenotyping of plasma cells across the spectrum of plasma cell proliferative disorders: A fresh insight with pattern-based recognition
papers.authors: Nupur Das, Meetu Dahiya, Ritu Gupta, Sandeep Rai, Saroj Singh, Vijay K. Prajapati, Lalit Kumar, Atul Sharma, Ranjit K. Sahoo, Ajay Gogia
papers.inclusion_reason: Recent large prospective series (791 samples) proposing pattern-based recognition of aberrant plasma cells using CD19/CD56/CD27/CD117/CD81/cyKLR, a current authoritative update on the discriminator panel; no open PMC full text found.

papers.title: Proposals for the immunological classification of acute leukemias. European Group for the Immunological Characterization of Leukemias (EGIL)
papers.authors: M C Bene, G Castoldi, W Knapp, W D Ludwig, E Matutes, A Orfao, M B van't Veer
papers.inclusion_reason: Foundational pre-Bethesda consensus scoring system (EGIL) that first standardized immunophenotypic lineage assignment and biphenotypic leukemia criteria, the direct historical predecessor to Bethesda 2006; no open-access equivalent exists so the paywalled PubMed record is included.

papers.title: 2006 Bethesda International Consensus recommendations on the immunophenotypic analysis of hematolymphoid neoplasia by flow cytometry: optimal reagents and reporting for the flow cytometric diagnosis of hematopoietic neoplasia
papers.authors: Brent L Wood, Maria Arroz, David Barnett, Joseph DiGiuseppe, Bruce Greig, Steven J Kussick, Teri Oldaker, Mark Shenkin, Elizabeth Stone, Paul Wallace
papers.venue: Cytometry Part B: Clinical Cytometry, 72 Suppl 1:S14-22
papers.inclusion_reason: The core 2006 Bethesda Consensus document defining consensus reagent panels and reporting standards -- the single most cited standardization document in clinical flow immunophenotyping; no open-access mirror found so the paywalled PubMed record is included.

papers.title: EuroFlow antibody panels for standardized n-dimensional flow cytometric immunophenotyping of normal, reactive and malignant leukocytes
papers.authors: J J M van Dongen, L Lhermitte, S Böttcher, J Almeida, V H J van der Velden, J Flores-Montero, A Rawstron, V Asnafi, Q Lécrevisse, P Lucio, E Mejstrikova, T Szczepański, T Kalina, R de Tute, M Brüggemann, L Sedek, M Cullen, A W Langerak, A Mendonça, E Macintyre, M Martin-Ayuso, O Hrusak, M B Vidriales, A Orfao; EuroFlow Consortium (EU-FP6, LSHB-CT-2006-018708)
papers.inclusion_reason: The founding EuroFlow consortium paper defining the validated 8-color standardized antibody panels and Infinicyt-based design methodology that underpin the current standard-of-care in clinical immunophenotyping; open access on PMC.

papers.title: Validation of cell-based fluorescence assays: practice guidelines from the ICSH and ICCS - part V - assay performance criteria
papers.authors: Brent Wood, Dragan Jevremovic, Marie C Béné, Ming Yan, Patrick Jacobs, Virginia Litwin; ICSH/ICCS Working Group
papers.inclusion_reason: Core ICSH/ICCS joint validation-guideline document (assay performance criteria) governing how clinical flow immunophenotyping assays must be validated for accreditation; no open-access mirror found.

papers.title: Standardized flow cytometry for highly sensitive MRD measurements in B-cell acute lymphoblastic leukemia
papers.authors: Prisca Theunissen, Ester Mejstrikova, Lukasz Sedek, Alita J van der Sluijs-Gelling, Giuseppe Gaipa, Marius Bartels, Elaine Sobral da Costa, Michaela Kotrová, Michaela Novakova, Edwin Sonneveld, Chiara Buracchi, Paola Bonaccorso, Elen Oliveira, Jeroen G Te Marvelde, Tomasz Szczepanski, Ludovic Lhermitte, Ondrej Hrusak, Quentin Lecrevisse, Georgiana Emilia Grigore, Eva Froňková, Jan Trka, Monika Brüggemann, Alberto Orfao, Jacques J M van Dongen, Vincent H J van der Velden; EuroFlow Consortium
papers.inclusion_reason: EuroFlow's standardized 8-color panel achieving RQ-PCR-comparable sensitivity for B-ALL MRD, a landmark demonstration that consortium standardization translates into clinically actionable sensitivity; open access on PMC (PMC5291958).

papers.title: Next Generation Flow for highly sensitive and standardized detection of minimal residual disease in multiple myeloma
papers.authors: J Flores-Montero, L Sanoja-Flores, B Paiva, N Puig, O García-Sánchez, S Böttcher, V H J van der Velden, J-J Pérez-Morán, M-B Vidriales, R García-Sanz, C Jimenez, M González, J Martínez-López, A Corral-Mateos, G-E Grigore, R Fluxá, R Pontes, J Caetano, L Sedek, M-C Del Cañizo, J Bladé, J-J Lahuerta, C Aguilar, A Bárez, A García-Mateo, J Labrador, P Leoz, C Aguilera-Sanz, J San-Miguel, M-V Mateos, B Durie, J J M van Dongen, A Orfao
papers.inclusion_reason: Defines Next Generation Flow (NGF), the EuroFlow/International Myeloma Foundation standardized next-generation MRD approach now the flow-cytometry gold standard for multiple myeloma MRD; open access on PMC.

papers.title: CD Nomenclature 2015: Human Leukocyte Differentiation Antigen Workshops as a Driving Force in Immunology
papers.authors: Pablo Engel, Laurence Boumsell, Robert Balderas, Armand Bensussan, Valter Gattei, Vaclav Horejsi, Bo-Quan Jin, Fabio Malavasi, Frank Mortari, Reinhard Schwartz-Albiez, Hannes Stockinger, Menno C van Zelm, Heddy Zola, Georgina Clark
papers.inclusion_reason: Authoritative history and current status of the CD nomenclature system as maintained by the HLDA/HCDM workshops -- the naming backbone every antibody panel in this field is built on.

papers.title: The International Consensus Classification of Mature Lymphoid Neoplasms: a report from the Clinical Advisory Committee
papers.authors: Elias Campo, Elaine S Jaffe, James R Cook, Leticia Quintanilla-Martinez, Steven H Swerdlow, Kenneth C Anderson, et al.
papers.inclusion_reason: The 2022 International Consensus Classification (ICC), the rival/complementary framework to WHO-5 that clinical flow immunophenotyping reports must map onto for mature lymphoid neoplasms; open access on PMC.

papers.title: The 5th edition of the World Health Organization Classification of Haematolymphoid Tumours: Myeloid and Histiocytic/Dendritic Neoplasms
papers.authors: Joseph D Khoury, Eric Solary, Oussama Abla, Yassmine Akkari, Rita Alaggio, Jane F Apperley, et al.
papers.inclusion_reason: The current WHO-5 classification for myeloid neoplasms that dictates which lineage-defining antigens a diagnostic flow panel must resolve; open access on PMC.

papers.title: Beyond the Limits: How Is Spectral Flow Cytometry Reshaping the Clinical Landscape and What Is Coming Next?
papers.authors: Kamila Czechowska, Diana L Bonilla, Adam Cotty, Amay Dankar, Paul E Mead, Veronica Nash
papers.inclusion_reason: Recent comprehensive review of spectral flow cytometry's clinical implementation, standardization challenges, and regulatory status -- covers the current high-dimensional/next-generation technology frontier of the subarea; open access on PMC.

papers.title: The ISHAGE guidelines for CD34+ cell determination by flow cytometry. International Society of Hematotherapy and Graft Engineering
papers.authors: D R Sutherland, L Anderson, M Keeney, R Nayar, I Chin-Yee
papers.inclusion_reason: Foundational, field-defining consensus protocol for CD34+ HSPC enumeration by flow cytometry; the sequential Boolean CD45/SSC/CD34 gating strategy it establishes remains the basis of clinical stem-cell gating today. Only available paywalled/abstract on PubMed (predates PMC deposit mandates); no open-access full-text equivalent found.

papers.title: Single platform flow cytometric absolute CD34+ cell counts based on the ISHAGE guidelines
papers.authors: M Keeney, I Chin-Yee, K Weir, J Popma, R Nayar, D R Sutherland
papers.inclusion_reason: Direct methodological successor to the 1996 ISHAGE guidelines: introduces 7-AAD viability dye gating and single-platform bead-based absolute counting, the version of the ISHAGE protocol still used clinically today. Paywalled/abstract-only on PubMed; no open-access equivalent found.

papers.title: Flow cytometry CD45 gating for immunophenotyping of acute myeloid leukemia
papers.authors: F Lacombe, F Durrieu, A Briais, P Dumain, F Belloc, E Bascans, J Reiffers, M R Boisseau, P Bernard
papers.inclusion_reason: Foundational validation of CD45/side-scatter blast gating (Loken-derived approach) against morphologic blast counts, showing CD45/SSC gating substantially outperforms FSC/SSC gating -- the basis for essentially all subsequent blast-gating methodology. Paywalled/abstract on PubMed; no open equivalent found.

papers.title: A dissection of the CD45/side scatter "blast gate"
papers.authors: Alexandra M Harrington, Horatiu Olteanu, Steven H Kroft
papers.inclusion_reason: Rigorous critical dissection of the standard CD45/SSC blast gate's composition and limitations across MDS, MPN, CMML and control marrows, quantifying non-blast contamination -- essential nuance/limitation counterpoint to the foundational Lacombe/Loken gating papers. Paywalled on Oxford Academic; no open equivalent found.

papers.title: 9-color and 10-color flow cytometry in the clinical laboratory
papers.authors: Brent L Wood
papers.inclusion_reason: Foundational methodological paper by Brent Wood establishing high-parameter (9-/10-color) flow cytometric approaches for blast/progenitor immunophenotyping and enumeration that underlie modern clinical bone marrow panels, including progenitor markers CD34/CD117/CD38/HLA-DR. Paywalled/abstract on PubMed; no open equivalent found.

papers.title: Multicenter prospective evaluation of diagnostic potential of flow cytometric aberrancies in myelodysplastic syndromes by the ELN iMDS flow working group
papers.authors: Wolfgang Kern, Theresia M Westers, Frauke Bellos, Matteo G Della Porta, Uta Oelschlaegel, Michaela Fuchs, Kim de Jong, Peter Valent, Robin Ireland, Chiara Cuteri, Anna Porwit, Sergio Matarraz, Alberto Orfao, Vincent H J van der Velden, Marie C Bene, Alan Saul Wagner, Peter Bettelheim, Michael R Loken, Katherina Psarra, Ulrika Johansson, Leonie Saft, Frank Preijers, Marion Subira, Christa Fonteijn, Canan Alhan, Sylvia Snauwaert, Peter M Voorhoeve, Denise A Wells, Sylvie D Freeman, Arjan A van de Loosdrecht
papers.inclusion_reason: Large prospective multicenter validation defining the >=3% myeloid progenitor threshold and a core 17-marker set (including CD34, CD117, HLA-DR, CD38, CD123) for identifying abnormal progenitor immunophenotype patterns in MDS -- the current authoritative standard distinguishing normal vs abnormal progenitor gating. Paywalled on Wiley; no open-access equivalent found despite search.

papers.title: Immunophenotyping myelodysplastic neoplasms: the role of flow cytometry in the molecular classification era
papers.authors: Evgenia Verigou, Theodora Chatzilygeroudi, Vasileios Lazaris, Anne-Lise de Lastic, Argiris Symeonidis
papers.inclusion_reason: Recent (2024) open-access authoritative review synthesizing progenitor-cell immunophenotyping markers (CD34, CD117, CD38, HLA-DR, CD123) and normal-vs-abnormal progenitor patterns for MDS diagnosis, tying the classical gating literature to current molecular classification. Open-access PMC full text.

papers.title: Reporting blast percentage for response assessment in acute leukemias: recommendations from an EHA/ELN expert panel
papers.authors: Sa A Wang, Leonor Arenillas, Francesco Buccisano, Monika Bruggemann, Wolfgang Kern, Manuel Menes, Adriana Plesa, Louisa Stone, Dominique Wellnitz, David A Westerman, Brent L Wood, Sylvie D Freeman
papers.inclusion_reason: Current (2026) consensus recommendations from EHA/ELN on standardized blast enumeration methodology across morphology, flow cytometry and MRD assays -- directly extends the Wood/ISHAGE gating lineage into present-day harmonized clinical reporting standards for blast percentage. Open-access on Haematologica.

papers.title: Two subsets of memory T lymphocytes with distinct homing potentials and effector functions
papers.authors: Federica Sallusto, Danielle Lenig, Reinhold Förster, Martin Lipp, Antonio Lanzavecchia
papers.inclusion_reason: Foundational paper defining central memory (CCR7+) vs effector memory (CCR7-) T-cell subsets, the basis for CD45RA/CCR7-based T-cell subset immunophenotyping used throughout clinical flow cytometry; paywalled at Nature with no open PMC equivalent found, but bibliographic data verified via Crossref.

papers.title: The Naming of Memory T-Cell Subsets
papers.authors: Stephen C. Jameson
papers.venue: Cold Spring Harbor Perspectives in Biology, 13(1):a037788
papers.inclusion_reason: Authoritative recent perspective reconciling the proliferation of naive/TCM/TEM/TEMRA/Tscm nomenclature (CD45RA, CCR7, CD27, CD28, CD95) that underlies modern T-cell subset gating schemes; open-access PMC full text.

papers.title: CD127 expression inversely correlates with FoxP3 and suppressive function of human CD4+ T reg cells
papers.authors: Weihong Liu, Angela L. Putnam, Zhou Xu-Yu, Gregory L. Szot, Michael R. Lee, Sara Zhu, Peter A. Gottlieb, Alberto Kapranov, Kevin G. Becker, Steven B. Wagner, Nancy S. Ferrara, Jeffrey A. Bluestone
papers.inclusion_reason: Foundational paper establishing CD4+CD25+CD127low/- as the standard flow cytometric definition of regulatory T cells, now used ubiquitously in place of intracellular FoxP3 staining for live Treg identification; open-access PMC full text.

papers.title: Single Antibody Detection of T-Cell Receptor αβ Clonality by Flow Cytometry Rapidly Identifies Mature T-Cell Neoplasms and Monotypic Small CD8-Positive Subsets of Uncertain Significance
papers.authors: Min Shi, Dragan Jevremovic, Gregory E. Otteson, Michael M. Timm, Horatiu Olteanu, Pedro Horna
papers.inclusion_reason: The pivotal Mayo Clinic clinical validation study establishing single-antibody TRBC1 flow cytometry as a rapid, robust method for Talphabeta-cell clonality assessment, now standard of care in T-cell lymphoma/leukemia workup; no open-access full text found, PubMed record used for verified bibliographic data.

papers.title: Anti-TRBC1 Antibody-Based Flow Cytometric Detection of T-Cell Clonality: Standardization of Sample Preparation and Diagnostic Implementation
papers.authors: Anton W. Langerak, Sandra de Bruin, Rik A. Brooimans, Wilma Sonneveld, Grazyna Kubalova, Melanie Verkerk, Vincent H. J. van der Velden, Jacques J. M. van Dongen
papers.inclusion_reason: Multicenter standardization study of the anti-TRBC1 (JOVI-1-derived) flow cytometric assay for Talphabeta clonality, defining validated staining protocols and analytic sensitivity now used across diagnostic labs; open-access PMC full text.

papers.title: Dual T-cell constant β chain (TRBC)1 and TRBC2 staining for the identification of T-cell neoplasms by flow cytometry
papers.authors: Pedro Horna, Matthew J. Weybright, Mathieu Ferrari, Dennis Jungherz, YaYi Peng, Zulaikha Akbar, F. Tudor Ilca, Gregory E. Otteson, Jansen N. Seheult, Janosch Ortmann, Min Shi, Paul M. Maciocia, Marco Herling, Martin A. Pule, Horatiu Olteanu
papers.inclusion_reason: Recent advance refining TRBC1-based clonality assessment by adding a complementary TRBC2 reagent, improving resolution of clonal T-cell populations and reducing false calls; Blood Cancer Journal is a Nature-portfolio open-access journal.

papers.title: Flow cytometric immunophenotypic profiles of mature gamma delta T-cell malignancies involving peripheral blood and bone marrow
papers.authors: Ejaz Ahmad, Douglas W. Kingma, Elaine S. Jaffe, Jeffrey A. Schrager, John Janik, Wyndham Wilson, Maryalice Stetler-Stevenson
papers.inclusion_reason: Foundational characterization of the flow cytometric immunophenotype (CD5, CD7, CD8, CD16, CD57) distinguishing gamma-delta T-cell LGL leukemia from hepatosplenic gamma-delta T-cell lymphoma; no open-access full text found, PubMed record used for verified bibliographic data.

papers.title: Flow cytometric and immunohistochemical characterization of the gamma/delta T-lymphocyte population in normal human lymphoid tissue and peripheral blood
papers.authors: Shuchan Wei, David P. Ryan, Barbara Lucas, Fu-Sheng Wang, Todd Sartor
papers.inclusion_reason: Establishes the normal-range flow cytometric appearance and immunophenotypic aberrancies used to distinguish reactive/benign gamma-delta T-cell populations from neoplastic ones, a key differential-diagnosis reference; open-access PMC full text.

papers.title: Flow Cytometric Pattern of TCRVδ Subtype Expression Rapidly Identifies γδT Cell Lymphoma
papers.authors: Ping Wang, Meng Wang, Xiaoli Zhang, Jing Ye, Xinxin Cao, Jian Li, Yan Zhang, Chunyan Ji, Daobin Zhou, Yan Zhu
papers.inclusion_reason: Demonstrates flow cytometric TCR-Vdelta subtyping (Vdelta1 vs Vdelta2) as a rapid discriminator between benign gamma-delta expansions and gamma-delta T-cell lymphoma, extending clonality assessment beyond alphabeta T cells; open-access Frontiers journal.

papers.title: Flow cytometry for the assessment of blood tumour burden in cutaneous T-cell lymphoma: towards a standardized approach
papers.authors: Maarten H. Vermeer, Helene Moins-Teisserenc, Martine Bagot, Pietro Quaglino, Sean Whittaker
papers.inclusion_reason: Consensus/standardization statement on flow cytometric B-staging of Sezary syndrome/mycosis fungoides using CD3/CD4/CD8/CD26/CD7/CLA markers to quantify circulating tumor burden, a core clinical application of T-cell immunophenotyping; open-access PMC full text.

papers.title: Monocytes and macrophages in flow: an ESCCA initiative on advanced analyses of monocyte lineage using flow cytometry
papers.authors: Lambert C, Preijers FWMB, Yanikkaya Demirel G, Sack U
papers.inclusion_reason: ESCCA society consensus/review on standardized flow-cytometric analysis of monocyte lineage maturation, complementing Matarraz 2017 with a broader European standardization perspective; marked free article on PubMed.

papers.title: Monocyte maturation pattern by flow cytometry expression of CD64, CD300e, and CD14 correlates to presence of myeloid neoplasm and helps identify blast equivalents in the setting of monocytic neoplasm
papers.authors: Zhang J, Kaplan J, Courville E
papers.inclusion_reason: Most recent authoritative update on monocyte maturation immunophenotyping, adding CD300e as a maturation marker alongside classical CD64/CD14 acquisition; no open-access equivalent found, included paywalled as the current state of the art.

papers.title: Early T-Cell Precursor Acute Lymphoblastic Leukemia: Diagnosis, Updates in Molecular Pathogenesis, Management, and Novel Therapies
papers.authors: Chun-fung Sin, Pui-hei Marcus Man
papers.inclusion_reason: Authoritative recent review of the early T-cell precursor (ETP-ALL) immunophenotype (CD1a-, CD8-, weak/absent CD5, myeloid/stem-cell marker co-expression) contrasted against cortical/typical precursor T-ALL, essential for T-lymphoblastic immunophenotyping; open-access PMC full text.

papers.title: The Flow Cytometric Evaluation of B- and T-Lymphoblastic Leukemia/Lymphoma
papers.authors: David M. Dorfman
papers.inclusion_reason: Up-to-date review of flow cytometric maturation-stage immunophenotyping of T-lymphoblastic leukemia/lymphoma (cCD3/CD7/CD2/CD1a/CD4/CD8/CD34/TdT staging from early precursor through cortical to medullary/mature thymocyte), complementing the ETP-ALL-focused review with the full precursor-to-cortical spectrum; open-access MDPI journal.

papers.title: Utility of CD26 in flow cytometric immunophenotyping of T-cell lymphomas in tissue and body fluid specimens
papers.authors: Diane M. Pierson, Dan Jones, Tariq Muzzafar, Marian J. Kersh, Pramoda Challagundla, L. Jeffrey Medeiros, Jeffrey L. Jorgensen
papers.inclusion_reason: Direct evaluation of CD26 loss as a T-cell lymphoma discriminator in tissue/body-fluid flow cytometry, complementing its established role in peripheral-blood Sezary/MF staging and clarifying its limitations as a standalone marker; no open-access full text found, PubMed record used for verified bibliographic data.
